ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass. The depicted attacker is an Endrider (a Wizards-internal Aetherdrift faction confirmed via MTG Wiki's 'Depicted in' list, which names Fuel the Flames as Endrider-faction art) and the cat-folk targets are unnamed generic figures. No external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (set code DFT, released 2025-02-14) is a multi-plane death-race set staging the Ghirapur Grand Prix across Avishkar (renamed Kaladesh), Amonkhet, and Muraganda, with ten competing teams [Scryfall API: dft set; Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1]. The set's vision design was led by Mark Rosewater and set design by Yoni Skolnik; codename was 'Tennis' [MTG Wiki: Aetherdrift].
sources: Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: The attacker on this card is an Endrider — the black-red racing team from the dying plane Gastal. MTG Wiki explicitly lists Fuel the Flames under the Endriders' 'Depicted in' card cycle [MTG Wiki: Endriders]. The faction is described in Wizards' guide as scavengers and nomads who 'roar across permanently sunset-washed landscapes seeking rare oases to settle or raid' on a homeworld where 'gas and oil are plentiful' but water is scarce; after the Phyrexian invasion they 'rode their machines to freedom, leaving Gastal without a look in the rearview mirror' and now do mercenary work across the Multiverse to keep their tanks full [Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1, via Miguel Lopez 2024-12-10]. MTG Wiki frames them as 'nomadic apocalypse punk warriors' [MTG Wiki: Endriders, citing the 'Buckle Up for a Race Across the Multiverse' Aetherdrift Debut video].
sources: Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text reads: "Our world died, but we survived. Funny how it's the opposite for you." —Rido, Endrider [Scryfall flavor_text]. Rido is a confirmed member of Far Fortune's Endrider crew on the team roster alongside Mincemeat, Crash, and Wrench [MTG Wiki: Endriders, infobox 'Members' list]. The line frames the burning sequence: the Endriders are post-apocalypse survivors whose home plane already died, so their indifference to setting another team on fire is treated as gallows comedy.

Aetherdrift coverage repeatedly framed the Endriders as the set's most direct Mad Max homage — story author K. Arsenault Rivera cited Mad Max, Fast and the Furious, Redline, and Speed Racer as her main touchstones [Magic Untapped: 'An Interview With Aetherdrift Story Author K. Arsenault Rivera', via WebSearch snippet]. The plane's name itself reads as 'Gas + Pedal' [WebSearch snippet of Aetherdrift coverage]. Art on this card is by Nicholas Gregory [Scryfall artist field]. The card has no reprints — single printing in DFT [Scryfall prints_search].

A {2}{R} instant dealing 2 damage to each creature with Cycling {2} [Scryfall oracle_text]. Functionally a Pyroclasm variant at instant speed with built-in card filtering — Draftsim's Aetherdrift Limited review notes that cycling makes 'situationally powerful cards like Fuel the Flames much more playable' since it doesn't become a dead card in matchups where a board wipe isn't needed [Draftsim: 'The Ultimate Aetherdrift Limited Set Review', via WebSearch snippet]. EDHREC rank 16,075 places it outside Commander staple territory [Scryfall: edhrec_rank]. Standard / Modern / Legacy / Vintage / Commander legal; Pauper not legal [Scryfall legalities].

related_cards.note: Endriders 'Depicted in' cycle; flavor text quotes the Endriders chanting 'The claw!' as they raid another racer.
